scope: Audit only: determine whether current full Playwright e2e tests reflect the current application before changing tests.

authFinding.issue: The suite bootstraps one admin storage state with /api/auth/test-login, but many admin tests and API fixture helpers rely on that shared state for long production-like runs. API fixture calls through the request fixture still produced 401s.
authFinding.recommendation: Use test-login in production-like test stacks, but make admin auth fresh and explicit per test/fixture: add an authenticated API request context helper, and call ensureAdminSession before admin UI flows that mutate or depend on session state.

currencyFindings.finding: Detail-page tests still use main h1 as a unique title selector. Current detail pages intentionally allow h1 headings inside rendered body content after the TOC h1 restoration.

Local QA query flags are host-gated to localhost/127.0.0.1, but the production-like run used an internal Docker nginx hostname. Tests expecting __qaBroken or __qaEmpty behavior are not valid in that environment as written.
Some public tests assert exact mutable CMS content such as Introduction. The production-like database currently contains modified content, so these tests are checking seed state instead of behavior.

Work video hover/resize tests mostly reflect the current WorkVideoPlayer test IDs and expected no-custom-bar behavior, but full-run failures occur before those assertions because admin creation/auth fails.
Notion view tests still target existing Notion workspace test IDs, but the failing autosave cases are fixture/API auth failures rather than direct Notion UI mismatches.
Recording/batch visual specs depend on editor globals and large recording fixture flows. They should be separated from the release-blocking full e2e lane or updated after auth is stable.

validation: Read Playwright project config and auth bootstrap.
Read content fixture helpers that create admin data through /api/admin/*.
Compared representative failing specs against current work/blog detail pages, LocalQaQueryBoundary, WorkVideoPlayer, AdminDashboardCollections, and Notion workspace selectors.
Parsed the prior production-like full e2e JSON report and generated classified failure artifacts.
intentionallyNotChanged: No production code changed during this audit.
No Playwright spec or helper changed during this audit.
No Docker stack restarted during this audit.
recommendationOrder: Fix the e2e auth harness first: keep test-login enabled for production-like test stacks, add an authenticated API context for fixtures, and refresh admin UI session per test or per admin fixture group.

Update stale public selectors and environment assumptions: replace main h1 unique-title assertions with scoped title test IDs/locators, and either run QA flag specs on localhost/127.0.0.1 or gate them through an explicit test env flag.
Remove mutable seeded-content assumptions from production-like tests; assert structure/behavior or create isolated fixture data instead.
Only then rerun targeted groups: public detail/TOC, admin smoke, Notion autosave, works video upload/preview.
Keep recording/live/batch visual suites in an optional lane until their editor instrumentation and seed setup are updated.
finalRecommendation: Do not treat the current 209 failures as 209 app regressions. The full e2e suite is partially out of sync with the current code and partially blocked by auth/session fixture design. Stabilize auth and stale selectors before using full-suite timing as a reliable production-like signal.
noteOnClassification: The per-result classified-failures.json is a raw JSON-pass helper. The authoritative failure category/symptom totals come from the prior full-run failure CSV/summary because that parser preserved Playwright call-log symptoms for timeouts.
